name: Cult Ascent
description: A high approach toward a structure the locals call the Ascent — a tiered edifice of black stone that predates the cult that now occupies it. The corruption here is the densest and most structured of any region, channeled rather than wild.
ambient_lines: The path is too clean. No leaves, no fallen branches. Something maintains it.
Black stone markers line the ascent, carved with symbols the cult uses for their rites.
Figures in the distance, robed, moving with coordinated purpose. They don't acknowledge you.
The corruption here has texture — not ambient but directional, pulling toward the structure above.
A cultist encampment, quiet at midday. The fire burns something that smells sweet and wrong.
The mark on your champion's skin resonates here. Not painfully. Welcomingly.
Chanting from the structure above. Rhythmic, distant, entirely too coherent.
The stone beneath your feet is warmer than it should be at altitude.
A delegation of cultists passes. They lower their heads to you. You don't know what that means.
Offerings along the path — fresh, specific, arranged with care you usually associate with grief.
The air at this elevation should be thinner. Your breath feels full. The corruption is nutritive here.
Black banners on poles. Kasyrra's sigil. More of them than you've seen outside her direct presence.
A cultist approaches. Offers water. The skin smells clean. You take it or you don't.
The structure above is larger than it looked from below. You have been underestimating it for an hour.
The corruption doesn't suppress resistance here — it makes resistance feel irrelevant.
Terraced gardens built into the ascent slope. The plants are wrong-colored. The fruit is large.
A ritual space carved into the rock face — a perfect circle, smooth, with drainage channels.
You pass through a boundary marked by salt and ash. The corruption pressure doubles immediately.
The devotees here look healthy. Well-fed, clear-eyed. That's the most unsettling thing.
From this height you can see the Wolf-Road below. It looks very far away.

time_variants.dawn: The cult performs dawn rites at the structure's base. The chanting is extensive. You can go around it.
time_variants.morning: Morning sees the most cult activity — devotees moving between terraces, performing assigned tasks.
time_variants.midday: Midday meditation period. The ascent goes quiet. This is the safest passage window.
time_variants.evening: Evening rites are smaller and more private. You hear them through walls and closed doors.
time_variants.night: At night the structure's upper tiers are lit with a dark luminescence. The cult is always awake.

points_of_interest.name: The Ascent Gate
points_of_interest.desc: The formal entry to the structure — black iron worked into Kasyrra's sigil, large enough to ride through. The gate stands open. It has always stood open.

points_of_interest.name: The Corruption Well
points_of_interest.desc: A circular shaft in the courtyard floor, covered with a grate. The corruption that radiates from it is the source of the Ascent's density. No one has measured its depth.

points_of_interest.name: The Devotee Commons
points_of_interest.desc: Where the cult's lay members live. Clean, organized, surprisingly ordinary. The devotees here came willingly. Some of them are looking for something they actually need.

arrival_scene: The Cult Ascent makes its presence felt before you see it — the corruption shifts from ambient to channeled, from weather to architecture. When the structure finally comes into view above the tree line, your first impression is of something ancient wearing a newer coat of ideology. The black stone is older than Kasyrra. She found it. She moved in. The door is open.

connections.name: Kitsune Circuit
connections.desc: South, where the Kitsune posts begin again. The circuit's calm feels intentionally opposed to this place.

connections.name: Wolf-Road Edge
connections.desc: West and down, back to the main road. The corruption pressure drops steadily with distance.
